llm_reasoning: The location offers a suitable mean temperature (14.577°C) and zero frost risk, which are positive for wheat cultivation. However, the strongest limiting factors are the very low rainfall (246.74 mm) and high soil pH (8.06). The low rainfall will lead to severe water stress, while the high pH will cause nutrient deficiencies, particularly for phosphorus, iron, and zinc. Operational risks include significant yield reduction without intervention. Mitigation requires substantial supplemental irrigation, soil amendments to lower pH, and precise nutrient management, potentially including foliar applications, to achieve viable yields.
